snippets.title: Minimal argparse CLI with `__main__` guard
snippets.explanation: Thin CLI: parse → read → call `chunk_text` → print → return exit code. Works as `python -m ragapp.chunker doc.txt --max-tokens 256` and (via the next snippet) as `ragapp-chunk doc.txt`.

snippets.title: Register as an installed command
snippets.code:
# pyproject.toml
[project.scripts]
ragapp = "ragapp.cli:main"            # main multi-command CLI
ragapp-chunk = "ragapp.chunker:_cli"  # convenience single-purpose CLI
snippets.explanation: After `pip install -e .`, both `ragapp` and `ragapp-chunk` are commands on PATH. Pip generates platform-appropriate wrappers — no shebang gymnastics needed.

snippets.title: Sub-commands with argparse
snippets.code:
# src/ragapp/cli.py
import argparse
import sys
from ragapp import index, search, ingest


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="ragapp")
    subs = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)

    p_ingest = subs.add_parser("ingest", help="ingest documents")
    p_ingest.add_argument("path")
    p_ingest.set_defaults(func=lambda a: ingest.run(a.path))

    p_index = subs.add_parser("index", help="build the index")
    p_index.add_argument("--backend", choices=["faiss", "bm25"], default="faiss")
    p_index.set_defaults(func=lambda a: index.build(a.backend))

    p_search = subs.add_parser("search", help="search the index")
    p_search.add_argument("query")
    p_search.add_argument("-k", type=int, default=5)
    p_search.set_defaults(func=lambda a: search.run(a.query, k=a.k))

    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return int(args.func(args) or 0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
snippets.explanation: `set_defaults(func=...)` lets each sub-parser carry its own handler. `main()` becomes pure dispatch; everything else is in `ingest`, `index`, `search`.

snippets.title: Same CLI with Typer (zero boilerplate)
snippets.explanation: Typer turns type hints into argparse-equivalent flags + auto-generated `--help`. Same UX as the argparse version, ~3× less code.

snippets.title: Graceful Ctrl-C and clean errors
snippets.explanation: Convert expected errors to short stderr messages + specific exit codes; never dump tracebacks for user-facing errors. Ctrl-C exits with 130 per Unix convention.
